name: Cecil Scott
abstract: Cecil Scott (November 22, 1905 in Springfield, Ohio – January 5, 1964 in New York City) was an American jazz clarinetist, tenor saxophonist, and bandleader. Scott played as a teenager with his brother, drummer Lloyd Scott.

concerts.label: Jazz Concert in Memory of Sidney Bechet

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I studied with – Cecil Scott lived close to my house on the Hill, on Sugar Hill. So I used to go up to 165th Street and take lessons from him
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Snub Mosley
about_quotes.evidence: Cecil Scott, that's where I met him; that's where I met Cecil.

about_quotes.speaker: Art Hodes
about_quotes.evidence: I had Sandy Williams, Kaiser Marshall, Cecil Scott, Herb Ward, Henry Goodwin and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: The musicians other than Frank Newton that I did not know were ... the leader Cecil Scott, tenor, clarinet and director ... I went to live in Springfield which was the home town and headquarters for Lloyd and Cecil. Cecil Scott stayed in the same place where I stayed ... I had to bring him home.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Waters
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee does not mention Cecil Scott. The mention comes from a third party (PS) identifying Kenneth Roane as having played in a band with Cecil Scott. The interviewee only confirms the identity of Kenneth Roane.

about_quotes.speaker: Hayes Pillars
about_quotes.evidence: There was another band out of Springfield, Ohio, that used to play through Cincinnati, into Louisville, and this band was Cecil Scott. ... But back in the '20's, they were well known through that part of the country.
